channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
item.description: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Iran pushes back against Trump's deadline
item.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's top officials pushed back against President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

(Image credit: Ilia Yefimovich)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: She paid into Medicare for years. Trump's immigration policy will end her coverage
item.description: A provision in the GOP's One Big Beautiful Bill Act will make Rosa María Carranza and an estimated 100,000 other lawfully present immigrant seniors ineligible. Her once secure retirement is in question.
item.content:encoded:
A provision in the GOP's One Big Beautiful Bill Act will make Rosa María Carranza and an estimated 100,000 other lawfully present immigrant seniors ineligible. Her once secure retirement is in question.

(Image credit: Hiram Alejandro Durán)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa G. Sánchez

item.title: These blind students say their college blocked their education. A new rule could help
item.description: Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.
item.content:encoded:
Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.

(Image credit: Kristian Thacker for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Shingles can hit younger than you think. The vaccine can prevent excruciating pain
item.description: A reactivation of the virus that causes chickenpox, the illness can be miserable. Here's what to know about early warning signs, long-term symptoms and some surprising news about the vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
A reactivation of the virus that causes chickenpox, the illness can be miserable. Here's what to know about early warning signs, long-term symptoms and some surprising news about the vaccine.

(Image credit: triocean/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Milne-Tyte

item.title: Savannah Guthrie returns to the 'Today' show months after her mother's disappearance
item.description: Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy Guthrie, has not been seen since returning home from a family dinner the evening of Jan. 31.
item.content:encoded:
Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy Guthrie, has not been seen since returning home from a family dinner the evening of Jan. 31.

(Image credit: Dia Dipasupil)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump says Iran has until Tuesday night to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump is in a tight corner politically as he ramps up Iran war messaging, Artemis II crew readies for lunar flyby.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says Iran has until Tuesday night to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump is in a tight corner politically as he ramps up Iran war messaging, Artemis II crew readies for lunar flyby.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew readies for Monday's lunar flyby. Here's what you need to know
item.description: At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.
item.content:encoded:
At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.

(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: UCLA storms past South Carolina to claim its 1st NCAA women's basketball title
item.description: UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.
item.content:encoded:
UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.

(Image credit: Rick Scuteri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors
item.description: The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.
item.content:encoded:
The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.

(Image credit: Hector Retamal)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby
